title: SING SING
logline: A man serving life in Sing Sing prison finds purpose and transformation through a theater rehabilitation program while mentoring a volatile newcomer and fighting for his own freedom.
antagonistOrConflict: The prison system itself, Divine G's wrongful conviction, and the internal struggle between hope for freedom and acceptance of incarceration
tone: Intimate, humanistic, quietly hopeful yet unflinching about the realities of incarceration
themes: The power of theater as rehabilitation

actBreaks.description: Divine G and Divine Eye connect at the window overlooking the Hudson, marking Divine Eye's full commitment to the program and the deepening of their mentor-student relationship.

validation.ruling: Scene #31 (p25, 33%) is a confrontation at the window where Divine Eye challenges Divine G's leadership and Divine G defends the collaborative process over individual ambition. This irreversible clash fundamentally shifts their dynamic from tentative cooperation to open conflict, forcing both into new territory they cannot retreat from.

actBreaks.description: Divine G's parole hearing ends in denial, shattering his hope for freedom and forcing him to reckon with remaining in prison indefinitely.

validation.ruling: Scene #64 (p54, 70%) is Divine G's parole board hearing where commissioners review his murder conviction and clemency petition. This scene represents the ultimate test of Divine G's transformation and irreversibly determines his fate, fundamentally changing the story's stakes as we move into the final act where he must reconcile his denied freedom with his artistic purpose.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates on an intimate, character-driven three-act structure that prioritizes emotional transformation over plot mechanics. Act One establishes Divine G's dual life—his theater work and his fight for freedom—while introducing Divine Eye as a resistant force who will become central to G's journey. The first act break is subtle and relational rather than plot-driven: it's the moment Divine Eye truly opens up at the window, signaling his transformation has begun. Act Two deepens both men's arcs through the rehearsal process, building toward Divine G's parole hearing—the structural hinge of the entire film. The denial at scene 64 is devastating precisely because we've watched G maintain hope throughout. Act Three deals with aftermath and acceptance: Divine Eye's parole is granted while G remains, inverting their positions and forcing G to find meaning beyond his own freedom. The structure mirrors the prison experience itself—time moves differently, small moments carry enormous weight, and traditional plot beats are replaced by incremental emotional shifts.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately patient, with an average scene length of 1.3 pages reflecting the rhythms of prison life—repetitive, incremental, punctuated by rare moments of intensity. The script front-loads exposition efficiently (scenes 3-8 establish the world), then settles into a slower, observational rhythm during rehearsals. A montage at scene 43 compresses time as the play comes together, accelerating toward the performance. The parole hearing (scene 64) is the longest sustained sequence, its deliberate pacing creating excruciating tension. After this climax, the script slows again, spending time with Divine Eye's departure and the seven-year time jump, before the brief, emotionally resonant final scenes (90-93) provide release.

incitingIncident.description: Divine G pitches his drama Fine Print about a record producer, but the group votes it down in favor of a comedy with time travel, pirates, and gladiators, leaving Divine G visibly disappointed.

validation.ruling: Scene #10 is merely Divine Eye brushing off Divine G to handle something else—no stakes are established. Scene #15 (p12, 16%) is the true inciting incident: Brent announces RTA will stage a comedy time-travel play instead of Divine G's serious work, fundamentally disrupting Divine G's plans and forcing him into a new collaborative dynamic that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: Divine Eye's monologue lands flat, and when Brent asks him to enter from the wing, he walks onstage timidly until Brent tells him to stop asking permission and own the stage like Hamlet owns the castle.

validation.ruling: Scene #41 (p41, 53%) marks the shift from reactive to proactive: Divine G steps in to coach Divine Eye on owning the stage, teaching him to enter 'big' and claim his presence. This moment of mentorship transforms their relationship and demonstrates Divine G's evolution from territorial artist to collaborative leader, raising the emotional stakes of their partnership.

climax.description: Divine G visits Divine Eye's completely empty cell to say goodbye, and Divine Eye insists it's not goodbye because Divine G will be out soon, but Divine G says he's good here and tells Divine Eye to keep painting.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. DIVINE EYE'S CELL - A LITTLE LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #64 is the parole hearing—a crucial moment but not the climax (it's already designated as Act Break 2). Scene #86 (p71, 92%) is the true climax: Divine G visits Divine Eye in his cell days before Eye's release, and they share a deeply vulnerable moment where Divine G reveals his parole denial and Divine Eye offers profound emotional support, resolving the central relationship arc and demonstrating both characters' complete transformations.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - DAY
scenes.summary: A man performs a monologue from A Midsummer Night's Dream on stage in a spotlight, surrounded by artificial trees and butterflies.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: The performer changes from costume into a green jumpsuit backstage while corrections officers watch, and the cast lines up to be counted by their numbers.

scenes.heading: EXT. SING SING CORRECTIONAL FACILITY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Establishing view of Sing Sing Correctional Facility on the Hudson River with its walls, razor wire, and a commuter train passing through the yard.

scenes.heading: INT. B BLOCK - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Divine G types at his desk in his cell in B Block during early morning while wearing headphones.

scenes.heading: INT. CELLBLOCK CORRIDOR - MORNING
scenes.summary: A line of incarcerated men moves through corridors as gates thunder open and shut around them.

scenes.heading: INT. MESS HALL - LATER
scenes.summary: A man approaches Divine G in the mess hall asking him to sign a copy of his book Money Grip, then awkwardly thanks him for writing it.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Divine G feeds geese in the yard from a baggie of vegetables, then drops to his belly with hands on his head when a siren wails.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: The Steering Committee meets in a classroom to discuss their recent production, sharing kudos and favorite moments including Divine G's monologue where he forgot lines.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD BERM - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G and Mike Mike scan the yard looking for someone in the heat, then Divine G spots him and they start walking toward him.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD COURTYARD, 2ND LEVEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Divine G approaches Divine Eye to speak with him, but Divine Eye says he needs to take care of something first and walks away to the lower courtyard.
scenes.turningPointNote: First encounter between Divine G and Divine Eye, establishing their dynamic and Divine Eye's character.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD COURTYARD, 1ST LEVEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Divine Eye tricks a young prisoner named Clay into holding a piece of tin foil all day, then takes it back and eats the candy inside while laughing at Clay's gullibility.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD COURTYARD, 2ND LEVEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Divine Eye returns laughing from his prank, and Divine G invites him to audition for RTA's next production, asking why he signed up for the program.

scenes.heading: EXT. HUDSON RIVER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of the Hudson River with Sing Sing's walls, guard towers, and a passing sailboat.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Fifteen men gather in the classroom for the theater program as Brent, a civilian volunteer with a ponytail and aviator pants, introduces himself and asks what play they want to do next.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G pitches his drama Fine Print about a record producer, but the group votes it down in favor of a comedy with time travel, pirates, and gladiators, leaving Divine G visibly disappointed.
scenes.turningPointNote: Divine G's play is rejected by the group, creating personal conflict and setting up the comedy they'll actually perform.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVINE G’S CELL - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G tries to repair his glasses in his cell while Mike Mike suggests from across the hall that they could make Fine Print funny or take another vote, but Divine G declines.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - ONLY DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Brent distributes scripts for Breakin' the Mummy's Code, a 147-page play he wrote over the weekend combining all the elements the group requested including dance numbers and musical interludes.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: A man auditions by performing an incredibly intense rendition of Happy Birthday, and Divine G writes only a question mark in his notes.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Multiple actors audition one after another with performances ranging from silly to profound, including acting scenes, telling stories, singing, dancing, and freestyle rapping.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Divine G finishes his intense audition for Hamlet, leaving the Committee speechless, then learns Divine Eye is also auditioning for the same role and is asked to leave.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G tells Divine Eye he's up for his audition, then asks why he's auditioning for the only dramatic role when he requested a comedy, and Divine Eye responds that comedy is tougher than drama.

scenes.heading: INT. REC ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G practices his parole hearing with Mike Mike role-playing as a commissioner, discussing Divine G's past as a DJ and his time hustling drugs.

scenes.heading: INT. REC ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Divine G continues the mock parole hearing with Mike Mike, who questions him about his DJ group Mix Machine while pretending to be a cop from an old movie.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Brent distributes roles to the cast on stage, with Divine G receiving his part quietly while Divine Eye searches for his, and one actor complains about being cast as a tree.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER / ONSTAGE - LATER
scenes.summary: An actor asks Brent questions about his character's motivation regarding time travel and language barriers with a mummy, but Brent tells him it's a comedy so they can take liberties.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER / ONSTAGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G rehearses a scene with Mike Mike, displaying skilled emotional performance while reading from the script, as Divine Eye watches barely paying attention from the wings.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER / ONSTAGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine Eye struggles through his scene, fumbling lines, losing his place, and complaining about not having glasses, until Brent helps him track lines with his thumb and they mercifully finish.

scenes.heading: INT. SING SING HALLWAY - LATER
scenes.summary: The Steering Committee waits in line discussing concerns about the play's complexity, props, wardrobe, and four-hour runtime, joking that B Block will murder them on stage.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: During a physical acting exercise where the group becomes objects, Divine Eye declines to participate, throwing off the group's energy before they move on.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G stops Divine Eye after class and asks to show him something, and Divine Eye reluctantly agrees after looking like he has somewhere else to be.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER / WINDOW - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Divine G leads Divine Eye to a window with a view of mountains and tries to offer help with the acting work, but Divine Eye rejects the help, saying he doesn't look where he can't go and isn't struggling.

scenes.heading: INT. MESS HALL - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G sits alone in the mess hall taking notes when he looks up to see Divine Eye dealing product and currency along a table, then returns to his work with growing frustration.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: The group does roll call in the theater and discovers Divine Eye is missing from rehearsal despite not being on keeplock, so they decide to continue without him.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVINE G’S CELL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A corrections officer conducts a random contraband check in Divine G's cell, turning over his bed, going through drawers, and destroying his meticulous order while Divine G waits outside.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Brent leads a meditation exercise asking the men to visualize their perfect spot, and Divine G notices Divine Eye arriving late and joining the circle.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Divine Eye struggles with blocking, getting nervous about someone walking behind him and forgetting his lines during his Hamlet soliloquy, until Brent calls for a break.

scenes.heading: INT. SING SING HALLWAY - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G waits in a long line stuck between gates in a hallway, clearly agitated, as a man yells to ask what's going on and a CO yells back for him to shut up.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G watches from the seats as actors struggle through a pirate ship dance number, then Divine Eye arrives soaking wet and they discuss the messy rehearsal process and trusting it will come together.

scenes.heading: INT. REC ROOM - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G shows Divine Eye his heavily annotated script and his method of writing scenes on a long scroll without punctuation, then memorizing in blocks, explaining the deep preparation required.
scenes.turningPointNote: Divine G and Divine Eye begin working together one-on-one, marking a shift in their relationship from tension to mentorship.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Brent directs a plague death scene that feels too stiff, then has everyone pair up for an exercise where they must die for each other, and Divine G reluctantly pairs with Divine Eye.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine Eye's monologue lands flat, and when Brent asks him to enter from the wing, he walks onstage timidly until Brent tells him to stop asking permission and own the stage like Hamlet owns the castle.
scenes.turningPointNote: Brent's direction about owning the stage represents a breakthrough moment for Divine Eye's character and performance.

scenes.heading: INT. REC ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: The group rehearses lines together in the rec room as the camera moves around them.

scenes.heading: INT. CELLBLOCK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Men rehearse between cells after lights out in the cellblock.

scenes.heading: INT. MESS HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Men rehearse across trays during chow in the mess hall.

scenes.heading: EXT. WEIGHT POUND - DAY
scenes.summary: Men rehearse between reps at the weight pound.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Brent fields questions from actors about props and creative choices, including complaints about a Phoenician headband, broccoli instead of a six-shooter, and Freddy Krueger's costume.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER / ONSTAGE
scenes.summary: Stage crew adjusts a spotlight that's off Divine Eye's mark, and when asked to move, Divine Eye initially resists saying he's on his mark, then agrees to move it.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY OUTSIDE THEATER - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Brent tells Divine G, Mike Mike and others that the Superintendent has wealthy donors watching and they need to make the fight scene savage to get funding for curtains.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER / ONSTAGE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G and Mike Mike perform a slow-motion gladiator fight with fake swords for the Superintendent and church women, who give polite applause, and the Superintendent later shakes his head at Brent.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: During lunch at the theater, Divine Eye tells Divine G he's struggling with Hamlet's soliloquy because his real pain is internal and make-believe won't change that he's still a gangster when he gets out.

scenes.heading: INT. ONSTAGE - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Brent announces the cast will dance instead of taking a traditional curtain call, and after a hesitant start Divine Eye leads everyone into freestyling down the line.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: The men unveil and play with the sets including a Roman chariot and rolling wave boards that transport everyone watching.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine Eye performs his soliloquy flawlessly until he stumbles on the very last line, and the company celebrates his progress despite the mistake.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G gives Divine Eye a folder of prep work for his parole hearing, but Divine Eye angrily rejects the help and walks away after telling Divine G to stop trying to save him.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVINE G'S CELL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Divine G and Mike Mike share stories across their cells about their childhoods, with Divine G opening up about dancing ballet at FAME school before Mike Mike falls silent.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIVINE G'S CELL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Divine G calls out to Mike Mike for not responding, then playfully says goodnight and rolls over.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVINE G'S CELL - NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Divine G lies staring at the wall while a janitor unceremoniously cleans out Mike Mike's cell, ripping down photos and bagging his belongings.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mike Mike's death (implied by the cell being cleaned out) is a major loss for Divine G and shifts the emotional landscape of the story.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - SOME TIME LATER
scenes.summary: The cast sits in a circle with one empty chair for Mike Mike, sharing stories about him and their grief while Divine G struggles to engage.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Divine Eye offers to talk about Mike Mike's death, but Divine G snaps at him defensively before softening and saying he needs to write a dozen letters.

scenes.heading: INT. REC ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G watches TV with other men in the common area.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD COURTYARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G circles the courtyard repeatedly for hours.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LIGHTING BOOTH - DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G watches from the lighting booth as men rehearse waltzing on the pirate ship set below.

scenes.heading: INT. PAY PHONE - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G talks on the pay phone with his mother, growing frustrated as she won't take action about something, then apologizes and admits he's anxious about an upcoming event.

scenes.heading: INT. PAROLE BOARD HEARING - DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G sits before three parole commissioners who review his convictions and dismiss his request to consider a letter from the Jeffrey Deskovic Foundation, telling him his clemency application has been denied.
scenes.turningPointNote: Divine G's clemency denial is a devastating blow to his hope for freedom and represents a major emotional crisis.

scenes.heading: INT. PACKAGE ROOM - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Divine G receives a thick letter from the Parole Board and immediately knows it's a denial without opening it.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER / WINDOW - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G finds Divine Eye at the window holding a single-page letter granting him parole, and when Divine Eye asks about Divine G's hearing, Divine G lies and says he hasn't heard yet.
scenes.turningPointNote: Divine Eye's parole approval contrasts sharply with Divine G's denial, creating a painful divergence in their fates and forcing Divine G to hide his devastation.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: The group celebrates Divine Eye's parole with a cupcake and stories, while Divine G hangs back in the wings watching as Divine Eye quotes something he read in solitary confinement.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Katherine brings in costumes for the first time and the cast tries them on with excitement, practicing in their eccentric outfits while she reminds them to account for every button and zipper.

scenes.heading: INT. ONSTAGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Brent gives a pep talk before final dress rehearsal and moments from the play unfold onstage while Divine G's mind seems elsewhere.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - ANCIENT EGYPT SET
scenes.summary: The Egyptian set is wheeled in and the prince performs his Egyptian slide while guards stand beside Mummy's sarcophagus.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - WHISKERANDOS TOWER
scenes.summary: Leslie and Maid Marion walk in place as Whiskerandos Tower is wheeled closer, revealing Whiskerandos who delivers his big line before a remote-controlled car brings a clue.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - FREDDY KRUEGER
scenes.summary: A shadow puppet of Freddy Krueger looms closer.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - HAMLET
scenes.summary: Divine Eye finishes his Hamlet soliloquy and the bizarre turns of Mummy's Code begin to unfold.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - PIRATE SHIP SCENE
scenes.summary: A bathtub sails across the sea opposite a pirate ship, then Leslie and Maid Marion waltz across the sea joined by pirates in Hawaiian shirts.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G delivers his gladiator scene lines fast and flat, throwing off his scene partners, then railroads through everyone's lines and tells Brent he's leaving the program.
scenes.turningPointNote: Divine G's decision to quit the program is a breaking point driven by his parole denial, abandoning the community that has sustained him.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - DAY
scenes.summary: The theater stage sits dark and empty, filled with props.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A classroom sits empty with chairs and lights out.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD - DAY
scenes.summary: The yard is empty with wind blowing dirt and wrens searching for scraps.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: The cast sits in a circle talking over each other about Divine G leaving, calling him a betrayer, until Dino cuts through and defends Divine G by reminding them he fought for each of them to be in the program despite their own breaking points.

scenes.heading: INT. MESS HALL - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Someone drops a wad of cash by Divine Eye's tray, and Divine Eye looks up to see Clay joining a group of hard-looking men, appearing ten years older and nodding in recognition.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVINE EYE'S CELL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Divine Eye sits in his cell surrounded by accumulated wealth of soup, shoes, and ramen, then pulls out his script and begins rehearsing his lines.
scenes.turningPointNote: Divine Eye chooses to return to the play and his artistic self rather than the material wealth he's accumulated, marking a shift in his values.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Brent leads a warmup where the cast imagines a friend, then reveals Charlie standing there in street clothes, and Charlie later shares his struggles with accepting love and removing the mask he wore in prison.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD / STAIRCASE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Divine Eye sits with Divine G on the stairs and confesses he lied about not knowing the plays when he joined, revealing he was artistic as a kid but didn't look like an artist, and tells Divine G he's the first person who saw him as an artist.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Divine G tells the cast in a huddle that he thought he was stronger and expresses gratitude for this family, and they welcome him back before starting an energy chant that builds into dancing.
scenes.turningPointNote: Divine G returns to the program and his community, choosing connection over isolation after his parole denial.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR - DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: A CO escorts Divine G down the cellblock and tells him the play was pretty good.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVINE EYE'S CELL - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G visits Divine Eye's completely empty cell to say goodbye, and Divine Eye insists it's not goodbye because Divine G will be out soon, but Divine G says he's good here and tells Divine Eye to keep painting.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM - SOME TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Divine G and the Steering Committee sit in the classroom discussing what play to do next, with members suggesting various titles while Divine G remains quiet.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainy footage shows seven years of curtain calls with Divine G and rotating casts in various costumes taking their bows.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVINE G'S CELL - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Seven years later, Divine G sits in his cleaned-out cell with a box and a light letter, then his gate opens and he steps out, nodding to a young man in Mike Mike's old cell before a CO walks him away.
scenes.turningPointNote: Divine G is finally released from prison after seven more years, achieving the freedom he was denied earlier.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE GATE - SOON AFTER
scenes.summary: Divine G stands between two steel gates with his paper sack, his heart thundering, as a CO checks his clipboard and signals for the second gate to open onto a neighborhood street where Divine G takes his first steps of freedom.

scenes.heading: INT. / EXT. PARKED SUV - DAY
scenes.summary: Divine Eye waits by his SUV and jokes with Divine G about worrying he'd decided to stay, and Divine G says he was trying not to walk too fast so they wouldn't think he was escaping.

scenes.heading: EXT. SING SING FENCE LINE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Divine Eye and Divine G embrace in a deep hug along the fence line with the prison and razor wire stretching out beyond them.

scenes.heading: INT. SUV / COUNTRY ROAD - LATER
scenes.summary: Divine Eye drives Divine G along a tree-lined road, and Divine G struggles with the overwhelming movement until Divine Eye tells him to sit with it, then Divine G leans back by the open window as farmland appears and he opens his eyes to the new world.
